title: aquilegia ottonis subsp. amaliae
intro: Aquilegia ottonis subsp. amaliae, also known as the Ottoman's columbine or Amalia's columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine, and it is reported to have medicinal properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Aquilegia ottonis subsp. amaliae are believed to include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe respiratory issues and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the roots to make a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
